sre/aws-cloud-native.name: AWS CloudWatch metrics and logs
sre/aws-cloud-native.why: Adds EC2, ECS, EKS, ALB, RDS, and Lambda health, saturation, and application logs.
sre/aws-cloud-native.how_to_ingest: Deploy the AWS Distro for OpenTelemetry Collector, configure CloudWatch collection, and export OTLP to Berserk. See https://aws-otel.github.io/docs/getting-started/collector/

sre/aws-cloud-native.name: AWS X-Ray traces
sre/aws-cloud-native.why: Adds distributed request paths and service dependency latency for AWS workloads.
sre/aws-cloud-native.how_to_ingest: Use an ADOT Collector trace pipeline and export the collected trace telemetry over OTLP to Berserk. See https://aws-otel.github.io/docs/getting-started/collector/

sre/aws-cloud-native.name: AWS CloudTrail
sre/aws-cloud-native.why: Adds AWS control-plane changes and identity activity; useful to both SRE and SOC lanes.
sre/aws-cloud-native.how_to_ingest: Route CloudTrail to CloudWatch Logs or S3, collect it with an OTel Collector log pipeline, and export OTLP to Berserk.

sre/azure.name: Azure Monitor and Log Analytics
sre/azure.why: Adds AKS, App Service, VM Insights, platform metrics, and Azure application telemetry.
sre/azure.how_to_ingest: Use Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry instrumentation or an OTel Collector/Azure Monitor pipeline, then export a second OTLP stream to Berserk. See https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/app/opentelemetry-configuration

sre/onprem-ad-health.name: Domain Controller Security events
sre/onprem-ad-health.why: Adds Kerberos 4768/4769/4771, account lockout 4740, logon, and privilege activity.
sre/onprem-ad-health.how_to_ingest: Run the OTel Collector on each domain controller with the windowseventlog receiver for the Security channel and export OTLP to Berserk. See https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector-contrib/tree/main/receiver/windowseventlogreceiver

sre/onprem-ad-health.name: Domain Controller System and Directory Service events
sre/onprem-ad-health.why: Adds NTDS replication, DFSR, netlogon, service, and directory health events.
sre/onprem-ad-health.how_to_ingest: Configure OTel Collector windowseventlog receivers for System and Directory Service channels on domain controllers, then export OTLP to Berserk.

soc/endpoint-identity.name: Sysmon endpoint telemetry
soc/endpoint-identity.why: Provides process, network, image-load, registry, and file activity needed for endpoint detections.
soc/endpoint-identity.how_to_ingest: Deploy Sysmon with a maintained full configuration, collect Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational using the OTel Collector windowseventlog receiver, and export OTLP to Berserk.

soc/endpoint-identity.name: Windows Security and identity events
soc/endpoint-identity.why: Provides authentication, account, group, Kerberos, and privilege events for identity detections.
soc/endpoint-identity.how_to_ingest: Collect Windows Security with the OTel Collector windowseventlog receiver; add Entra ID sign-in and audit logs through an Azure/Event Hub collector pipeline.

soc/endpoint-identity.name: DNS, firewall, proxy, and authentication logs
soc/endpoint-identity.why: Adds network and authentication context needed to correlate endpoint and identity behavior.
soc/endpoint-identity.how_to_ingest: Use OTel Collector filelog, syslog, or vendor receivers close to each source and export normalized OTLP logs to Berserk.

soc/endpoint-identity.name: AWS CloudTrail
soc/endpoint-identity.why: Provides AWS API, IAM, and control-plane activity for cloud identity detections.
soc/endpoint-identity.how_to_ingest: Route CloudTrail to CloudWatch Logs or S3, collect it with an OTel Collector log pipeline, and export OTLP to Berserk.

change-management/ansible.name: Ansible playbook and task telemetry
change-management/ansible.why: Adds change execution, task duration, failures, and remediation context without custom instrumentation.
change-management/ansible.how_to_ingest: Enable community.general.opentelemetry in callbacks_enabled, install opentelemetry-api/sdk/exporter-otlp-proto-grpc on the control node, and set OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT to Berserk ingest. See https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible/latest/collections/community/general/opentelemetry_callback.html

scom.name: System Center Operations Manager
scom.why: Adds SCOM alerts, health state, monitored object, and data-warehouse history to the shared telemetry view.
scom.how_to_ingest: SCOM has no native OTel receiver. Build a small read-only bridge from the SCOM REST API or OperationsManagerDW SQL to OTLP, then send it to Berserk. REST reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/rest/operationsmanager/
